hook: I'm about to show you why your guidance counselor didn't tell you this
concept: trade school in 6 months vs 4 years of debt
script: I'm about to show you why your guidance counselor didn't tell you this. [PAUSE] So your parents are probably pushing college, right? Four years, one hundred grand in debt, maybe a job that has nothing to do with your degree. [PAUSE] But here's what nobody talks about: you could be a licensed electrician, plumber, or HVAC tech in six months. [PAUSE] Six months. Most trade programs cost between three and fifteen grand total. [PAUSE] Meanwhile, your college roommate is paying forty grand a year and working at Target on the weekends. [PAUSE] In the time they're finishing sophomore year, you could already be making sixty to eighty grand annually—no debt, no student loans haunting you until you're forty. [PAUSE] The trades aren't a backup plan. They're the plan. [PAUSE] Check the link in bio for more at ihatecollege.com
